posture: Steady — community-benefit and logistics news, no action items.

items.headline: First $60m in New England community benefits unlocked
items.summary: The first tranche of community benefit funding tied to the New England REZ was announced.
items.so_what: A positive social-licence data point; track how allocation lands locally.

items.headline: Eligible benefit area expanded to towns outside the South West REZ boundary
items.summary: Coleambally, Jerilderie and Deniliquin were added as eligible for benefit funding despite sitting outside the zone boundary.
items.so_what: Boundary expansions can set precedent and invite further claims from adjacent communities.

items.headline: Golden Highway weekend closures scheduled for OSOM corridor works
items.summary: Planned weekend closures on the Golden Highway were flagged to accommodate corridor upgrade works.
items.so_what: Coordinated traffic messaging reduces community-complaint risk during disruption.

items.headline: ACEREZ reports local supplier and Aboriginal-business engagement on Central-West Orana
items.summary: The network operator reported a growing register of local and Aboriginal-owned businesses engaged on the transmission build.
items.so_what: Supports the local-benefit narrative underpinning social licence.

items.headline: Network operator procurement for New England progressing
items.summary: The network-operator process for New England continues after an earlier shortlist announcement.
items.so_what: Operator selection is a key delivery-confidence milestone for the zone.
